headlines.title: US and Iran agree to two-week ceasefire with Pakistani mediation
headlines.summary: The United States, Israel and Iran have agreed to a conditional two-week ceasefire, reached with Pakistani and Turkish mediation. U.S. and Iranian officials are expected to hold talks on Friday to discuss a long-term settlement.

headlines.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
headlines.summary: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war began.

headlines.title: Israel carries out large wave of air strikes across Lebanon
headlines.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

headlines.title: Russia and China veto watered-down UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Russia and China blocked a UN resolution aimed at reopening the critical Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnapping in Iraq
headlines.summary: American journalist Shelly Kittleson has been released one week after being kidnapped in Iraq, according to Secretary Rubio.

headlines.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
headlines.summary: Greece will ban social media for children under 15 starting next year, following similar moves in France and Spain.

headlines.title: Madagascar declares state of emergency over severe fuel shortages linked to Iran war
headlines.summary: The presidency says it is taking action as it fears fuel shortages linked to the Iran conflict could lead to public disorder.

headlines.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 distance record, flies over Moon's far side
headlines.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has broken the distance record set by Apollo 13 and successfully flown over the far side of the Moon.

headlines.title: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
headlines.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith, Australia's most decorated living soldier, faces five charges of the war crime of murder and will remain in custody.

headlines.title: IMF: Wars cause large and persistent economic losses, output declines 7% over five years
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund released research showing wars cause output to decline by roughly 7% over five years on average, with economic scars lasting more than a decade.
